Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Answers And Request For Sanctions;
Off Calendar Oct-02-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Stephen R. McCutcheon, Jr. Esq., for defendant and moving party: Seateq Corporation, et al
Anthony Washington, Esq., for Plaintiff and Opposing party: Anthony Morse

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendants Seateq Corporation, On Call Storge corp., Zipcubes Partners, Inc., Zipcubes Storage Corporation's Motion To Compel Deposition Answers is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is denied as to Question 1 as it is overly broad.
The motion is granted as to questions about tax matters as contained in matters designated Question Numbers 2, and 20-30. The previous order did not make a finding that all inquiry into matters relating to tax matters was irrelevant or privileged. The order on the previous motions was made on the request for tax returns, and reserved the option to renewing a request for those returns if other forms of discovery were insufficient.

As to Question Numbers 3-7, the motion is denied as the objections were proper. As to Question Numbers 9-19, the court finds that counsel improperly coached the witness.

The court orders that the continuation of the deposition be scheduled to be held in Room 612 on afternoons to be scheduled with the Clerk of the Discovery Department.

All requests for sanctions are denied. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

MOTION TO COMPEL INDENTIFICATION/PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
Off Calendar Nov-05-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Stephen R. McCutchen, Esq., appeared for Defendant & moving party: SEATEQ CORPORATION
Peter Washington, Esq., appeared for Plaintiff & opposing party: Anthony Morse

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Seateq Corporation's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as to Numbers 10 and 28. If no treatment was received as a result of defendant's acts, Plaintiff must make that verified response. If no documents have been prepared that relate to damages incurred as a result of defendant's acts, Plaintiff must make that verified response.
The motion is denied as to Number 11 as it invades Plaintiff right to privacy without good cause.
The motion is denied as to Numbers 13 and 14. The court does not find that there has been a showing of waiver or good cause. The court does not find that the gravamen of Plaintiff's lawsuit is so inconsistent with the continued assertion of the taxpayer's privilege as to compel the conclusion that the privilege has in fact been waived. It is possible that further discovery may reflect that such evidence can only be obtained from the tax returns, but that showing has not been made in this motion. Sanctions are denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
